topic: Part 6.13 The MOND Grouping — A Category-Error Import (section-new, EXP-295 P0 integration source review)
trigger_note: Queue item 382 is the section-new push for the Part 6.13 prose drafted in EXP-295. The five WIN reclassifications (047/048/052/054/055 -> Self-Contradicted) referenced by the prose have already been applied and adversarially reviewed under WIN-047.c3, WIN-048.c4, WIN-052.c3, WIN-054.c3, WIN-055.c3 (per HNOTE-OPERATOR-MOND-GROUPING-VERDICT-REVIEW-001-consolidation.json). This review attacks the section prose itself — the cross-cutting argument, the import-vs-convergence framing, the closed-world/dimensional argument, the placement-choice/self-own section, and citation integrity. Treats the proposed_subsection_html as section-new c1.
our_argument_summary: Part 6.13 frames the late-March 2026 dome-author addition of WIN-044 through WIN-054 as the import of the MOND-community canonical anomaly list (RAR, El Gordo, CMB Axis of Evil, Hubble tension, Cepheid tension, plus tidal constituents) without any dome-internal derivation. Argues that the dome's bounded firmament (disc_radius ~20,015 km; firmament_height ~9,086 km) cannot in principle accommodate the galactic-scale physics those anomalies are about (RAR's a_0 ~ 10^-10 m/s^2 only matters at 10^20 m scales; El Gordo at 10^25 m; CMB across the observable universe). Concludes the five cosmological WINs (047, 048, 052, 054, 055) are Self-Contradicted under the closed-world/category-error reading, locates the self-own in the dome author's choice of the WIN-confirmed category over his own internal 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label, and ends with a deeper-lesson observation that the dome cosmology cannot be smaller than its sources of fringe-science legitimacy.

The most-recent neighbor p6-614-kappa-cluster already broke the pattern toward descriptive-slug, so this is consistent with the newer trend but inconsistent with the older bulk.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Prose does not depend on dome code; it depends on per-WIN claim text in data/wins.json and the dome's published killshot.html structure. Verification done: (1) five reclassified WINs (047/048/052/054/055) all currently show verdict='Self-Contradicted' in data/wins.json — matches what the prose asserts; (2) WIN-052 cites Mistele et al. 2024 ApJL 969 L3 (DOI 10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0) and McGaugh et al. 2016 PRL 117.201101 — matches prose; (3) WIN-054 cites Asencio, Banik & Kroupa 2023 (DOI 10.3847/1538-4357/ace62a) for the 6.2-sigma El Gordo framing — matches prose; (4) WIN-048 detail_evidence explicitly contains the phrase 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' as a dome-internal sub-category label — matches prose's self-own argument; (5) WIN-029 detail_evidence contains all three firmament-height values 8,537 / 9,086 / 9,572 km — matches prose's 'three inconsistent peak values' claim; (6) data/kill-shots.json shows 7 entries but KS-007 (Stellar Parallax) is marked 'Not evaluated (our addition)' — the dome's actual killshot.html has the 6 rows the prose enumerates (Sydney-Perth, Polaris elevation, Eclipse Ebro, NMP drift, SAA decay, JFK-LHR slipstream). All citation-level claims pass.

dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in the prose itself; the prose closes the dome's likely 'two options' escape (galaxies are real -> firmament unbounded; galaxies are firmament artifacts -> measurements invalid) but leaves a third option implicitly open — galaxies as illusory 2D firmament patterns whose measurements probe the illusion mechanism, not dome physics. See Hole 3 below.

holes_found.description: Lead claim 'Eleven WINs (WIN-044 through WIN-054) imported in late March 2026 trace, almost item-for-item, to the modified-gravity / MOND community's published list of "anomalies LCDM struggles with"' is overgeneralized. The five cosmological WINs (047 Hubble, 048 CMB Axis of Evil, 052 RAR, 054 El Gordo, 055 Cepheid distance ladder) clearly do trace to the MOND-community list. But WIN-045/046/049/050/051 (tidal harmonic constituents M2/S2/K1/O1/N2) are NOT MOND-community talking points — tidal oscillations on Earth's surface are oceanographic/geophysics phenomena, well-explained by standard physics, that MOND does not address (MOND's domain is galactic-scale dynamics at acceleration ~a_0 ~ 1.2e-10 m/s^2, not coastal tides). Similarly WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry) and WIN-053 (two-pole geomagnetic model) are dome-internal derivations of local Earth phenomena, not MOND-community items. The prose later partially acknowledges this ('a custom-derived firmament scaling function bridging two of these (WIN-044, WIN-053)') but the lead 'almost item-for-item' claim still asserts the full eleven trace to the MOND list. A defender who clicks through and finds tidal phenomena listed as 'MOND-community talking points' has a legitimate gotcha: the prose conflates 'imported in the same commit burst' (true for all 11) with 'imported from the MOND-community canonical list' (true only for 5).
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the lead claim. Two options: (a) explicitly distinguish 'five cosmological WINs (047, 048, 052, 054, 055) trace item-for-item to the MOND community's canonical anomaly list; the other six WINs in the same commit burst — tidal constituents 045/046/049/050/051 and dome-internal derivations 044/053 — fall under different but adjacent patterns (standard physics relabel and dome-internal failed derivation respectively)'; or (b) narrow the framing to 'eleven WINs in the same commit burst, of which five trace to the MOND list'. Option (a) is more rigorous and matches the research note's own internal distinction.

holes_found.description: The 'import vs convergence' framing has its strongest evidence held back. The prose argues 'He imported them, in a single late-March commit sequence' but does not show the commit-burst evidence — three commits on 2026-03-23 at 04:57, 05:14, 05:29 UTC introducing 11 WINs in 32 minutes (research note documents this verbatim). A dome defender can legitimately respond 'how do you know it was import not independent convergent derivation? show me the import.' The prose has the answer in the operator's research note but does not surface it. The operator's methodology decision to keep the LLM-co-author commit-footer detail private is correct (avoids attacking methodology), but the timestamp/burst evidence is a strictly stronger argument that does NOT require the LLM detail — it's just commit dates and counts, which are public on the dome's repo. As written, the prose makes a load-bearing import claim and asks the reader to take it on faith.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence surfacing the public commit-burst evidence: 'Three commits to the dome's predictions repository on the morning of 2026-03-23 (04:57, 05:14, 05:29 UTC) introduced WIN-044 through WIN-054 in 32 minutes — eleven WINs in a single burst, not eleven independently-derived predictions.' This is the publishable subset of the research-note evidence; the LLM-co-author detail stays in the research note as the operator decided.

holes_found.description: The 'two options' framing on galaxies — either real (firmament unbounded) or firmament artifacts (measurements invalid) — does not explicitly close a third dome-defender move: galaxies as 2D illusory patterns on the firmament that nonetheless yield real measurement results when probed (the dome could argue 'galaxies are firmament features whose photons produce real spectra, and those spectra produce a real RAR-shaped relation when measured, without requiring 3D extragalactic mass distributions'). The prose implicitly handles this ('their measured properties cannot serve as evidence for the dome, because the measurements were of phenomena the dome cosmology denies exist'), but does not make the argument explicit: if galaxies are 2D firmament features, then RAR/El Gordo/Cepheid measurements probe properties of the illusion-generation mechanism, not properties of any underlying 3D mass distribution — which is fine for the dome's internal consistency, but means the data cannot count as confirmation of a cosmology in which galactic-scale physics is happening. The five WIN-level reviews (e.g., WIN-052.c3) address this more directly than the proposed section does.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a sentence after the existing 'two options' framing: 'A third reading the dome could attempt — galaxies as 2D firmament-projected patterns whose photon properties happen to fall along an RAR-shaped curve — does not escape the contradiction either: in that reading, the measured RAR is a property of the illusion-generation mechanism, not of any 3D mass distribution, and so cannot be cited as confirming a cosmology in which galactic-scale physics is the explanatory mechanism.' Or punt to the per-WIN reviews via cross-reference.

holes_found.description: The 'canonical MOND-community anomaly list' attribution lacks a specific citation. The prose names 'Stacy McGaugh maintains it in varying forms on his Triton Station blog; Pavel Kroupa and Indranil Banik in the Bonn group publish its components in their cosmology papers; it circulates in the MOND-curious sphere as the canonical set of anomalies.' Three names and one blog name with no URL, DOI, or specific publication. A dome defender can legitimately press 'where exactly is this list documented?' The individual DOIs in the prose (McGaugh 2016 PRL for RAR, Asencio 2023 for El Gordo) are for component papers, not for any community-curated list. This weakens the import-vs-convergence argument — without a pointer to a specific document where the canonical list is enumerated, the 'community-list' attribution rests on the reader's general impression of the MOND literature.
holes_found.recommendation: Cite at least one specific document where the anomaly list is enumerated. Plausible candidates: Banik & Zhao 2022 'From galactic bars to the Hubble tension: weighing up the astrophysical evidence for Milgromian gravity' (Symmetry 14 1331) which surveys MOND-relevant anomalies; or a McGaugh Triton Station post that explicitly enumerates 'the standard list'; or Kroupa et al. 2023 review papers. Pick one with a stable URL/DOI and one-sentence quote that establishes the list exists as a community artifact.

holes_found.description: Kernel-of-truth pivot is weaker than Part 6's established pattern. The decision_3 analyst commentary explicitly identifies the kernel — the dome author DID have a more accurate internal sub-category available ('LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label used on WIN-048) that, if applied consistently to the other four cosmological WINs, would have produced a coherent (if much weaker) claim structure ('the alternative is in trouble' rather than 'my model is right'). The proposed prose mentions this in the 'category placement, and the self-own' section but doesn't lead with it as kernel-of-truth in the Part 6.13 framing. Compare to e.g. p6-614-kappa-cluster's structure, which is more explicit about what the dome got right before pivoting to why it doesn't save the claim. The 'kernel' here — that the dome's own taxonomy contained a coherent place for this material and the author chose not to use it — is rhetorically strong but is buried.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a short 'kernel of truth' h3 paragraph near the top, explicitly: 'The dome author DOES have an internal sub-category for this kind of argument — the LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction label visible on WIN-048 — and that sub-category would have been the coherent home for these eleven items. The category-error here is not that the items are wrong to surface, but that they are surfaced under the strongest-claim category when the author's own taxonomy contained a more accurate one.' Or (b) leave as-is and accept that the self-own paragraph carries the kernel.

holes_found.description: Specific numeric claim 'firmament height function H(r) ranges from approximately 9,086 km at the apex (r=0) to approximately 8,619 km at the rim (r=20,015 km)' should be verified against the dome's actual H(r) formula. The number 8,619 km appears in WIN-053 as a scaling parameter (lambda) governing both H(r) and B(r), not as the explicit rim-height value. If the dome's H(r) is exponential with H_peak=9,086 km and characteristic length lambda=8,619 km, then H(20,015) = 9,086 * exp(-20,015/8,619) approximately 9,086 * 0.0982 approximately 892 km — orders of magnitude smaller than 8,619. If H(r) is the V51 ellipse form, the rim value could be very different. A reader checking the math can spot the inconsistency between '8,619 km at rim' and a typical exponential decay. The argument does NOT depend on the specific rim value (the dimensional incompatibility holds for any rim height below ~10^7 m), but the specific number invites a math-check distraction.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) replace with an unspecific statement: 'the firmament height function H(r) is bounded above by approximately 9,086 km across the full disc'; or (b) verify the rim value against the dome's actual H(r) formula evaluation (V51 ellipse form) and quote the correct number, citing the formula source.

holes_found.description: Subsection id format 'p6-613-mond-grouping' uses descriptive-slug; older Part 6 subsections use numeric-counter format (p6-61-0, p6-612-11). The most-recent neighbor p6-614-kappa-cluster already broke the pattern toward descriptive-slug, so the proposed id is consistent with the newer trend. No correction needed; flagged for awareness if/when the project decides to normalize id formats across Part 6.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. Flagged for structural consistency tracking only.

holes_found.description: Closing rhetorical generalization 'every nearby program in the modified-gravity / alternative-cosmology landscape ... requires the universe to be much larger than the dome allows' surveys three programs (MOND, plasma cosmology, modified-inertia). The claim survives strict scrutiny — the dome's ~10^7 m extent is geometrically incompatible with essentially any cosmological-scale alternative — but the prose presents it as a universal-quantifier statement after examining only three exemplars. A pedantic defender could press 'have you looked at conformal cyclic cosmology? ekpyrotic? steady-state?' Not realistic — the dome's smallness is the issue, not the specific alternative — but the universal framing invites the pushback. The analyst's kernel_of_truth field flagged this and recommended the 'neighborhood does not exist' close, which the prose adopts; this is a residual softness, not a fatal hole.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: tighten to 'each of the modified-gravity programs examined here ... operates at scales where the dome cosmology cannot exist' — closes the universal quantifier without weakening the rhetorical punch. Or leave as-is; the 'neighborhood does not exist' final sentence largely covers it.

holes_found.description: TLDR is on the dense side. Four sentences carrying: (a) the eleven-WIN import claim, (b) the convergence-vs-import distinction, (c) the closed-world dimensional argument, (d) the (data/wins.json:WIN-047,048,052,054,055; coordinates.html) citation block. Decision_3 explicitly noted the operator's TLDR concern and trimmed the placement-choice preview out. What remains is still heavier than typical Part 6 TLDRs — readers who scan TLDRs may not retain the convergence-vs-import distinction (which IS the most novel observation in the section). Not a blocker — Part 6.13 is a substantive cross-cutting argument that warrants a heavier TLDR than e.g. a single-WIN TLDR.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: split the closed-world argument out of the TLDR and let it land in the body. Resulting TLDR: 'Eleven WINs (WIN-044 through WIN-054) imported in late March 2026 trace, almost item-for-item, to the modified-gravity / MOND community's published list of anomalies LCDM struggles with. Independent convergence on this same list would be striking corroboration; importing it without a single derivation from disc-cosmology principles is borrowing, not convergence.' (Trimmed citation block too.) Or leave as-is; the current TLDR is acceptable for a structural-analysis subsection.

stronger_arguments.description: The publishable subset of the 32-minute commit-burst evidence (three commits at 04:57, 05:14, 05:29 UTC on 2026-03-23 introducing 11 WINs) makes the 'import not convergence' argument load-bearing in a way the current prose does not. The LLM-co-author footer detail stays in the research note; the timestamp data is on the public dome repo and is fair game.

A specific citation to a MOND-community paper that ENUMERATES the canonical anomaly list (e.g., Banik & Zhao 2022 Symmetry 14 1331 'From galactic bars to the Hubble tension') would harden the import-vs-convergence framing and close the 'where is this list documented' challenge from h4.
sources: Suggested external: Banik & Zhao 2022, Symmetry 14, 1331, DOI 10.3390/sym14071331 (or equivalent MOND-community review)

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-reference to the per-WIN reviews (WIN-047.c3, WIN-048.c4, WIN-052.c3, WIN-054.c3, WIN-055.c3) at the end of the prose would give a curious reader the audit trail — Part 6.13 is the cross-cutting summary, the WIN-level reviews carry the per-WIN argumentation. Currently the prose carries no such pointer.

An explicit closure of the 'galaxies as 2D firmament features' third-option defense (Hole 3) would foreclose the dome's likely tactical retreat: any reading that lets the dome keep cosmological measurements while denying cosmological space ultimately collapses RAR / El Gordo / Cepheid measurements to probes of the illusion-generation mechanism rather than to confirmations of disc cosmology.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-052 detail_evidence (already makes this argument at the per-WIN level)

kernel_of_truth.description: The analyst's own kernel field (EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json:kernel_of_truth) already documents what the section gets genuinely right and should be quoted nearly verbatim: the commit-history forensics, the convergence-vs-import distinction, the closed-world / square-cube argument, and the framework-level contradiction structure are correct. The category-error reading IS what justifies the SC reclassification (verified against current data/wins.json: WIN-047/048/052/054/055 all currently show verdict=Self-Contradicted). The decision to drop the kill-shot-tab 'anti-globe ammunition' framing — which was factually wrong against the dome's actual killshot.html (six side-by-side discriminating tests, verified against data/kill-shots.json items KS-001 through KS-006) — and replace it with the dome's own internal taxonomy (the 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' sub-label, verified against data/wins.json:WIN-048 detail_evidence) yields a tighter version of the same self-own observation. The 'fringe of real science still has empirical commitments incompatible with a closed-world dome' deeper-lesson is the section's rhetorical climax and is original to this review.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel is mostly intact — Hole 1 (overgeneralized eleven-vs-five lead), Hole 2 (held-back commit-burst evidence), and Hole 3 (third-option foreclosure) are tightenings rather than refutations. The section's verdict (Part 6.13 as a legitimate cross-cutting structural subsection in Part 6, parallel to p6-612-11 and p6-614-kappa-cluster) holds. The five per-WIN reclassifications it documents are already in place (verified against data/wins.json: 047/048/052/054/055 = Self-Contradicted). The recommendation is minor_edit — tighten Hole 1's lead claim and Hole 2's import evidence, optionally address Hole 3 and Hole 4 — not major_rewrite. None of the holes invalidate the structural argument or the category-error framework.

text_fingerprint.verdict: section-new (cross-cutting structural subsection of Part 6, parallel to p6-612-11 and p6-614-kappa-cluster)

deeper_analysis:
The proposed Part 6.13 is one of the strongest cross-cutting observations in the review — it identifies a specific structural pattern (a known external anomaly list imported as a group without per-item derivation) and traces it to a specific 32-minute window in the dome's commit history. The closed-world / square-cube dimensional argument is one of the cleanest formulations in the entire review of why the dome's bounded geometry forecloses certain physics in principle, not merely empirically. The category-error reading (closed-world commitments + galactic-scale referents = self-contradiction) is consistent with the existing Self-Contradicted verdict pattern (see data/wins.json: the verdict applies to both numerical/textual conflicts and to framework-level coherence failures, per WIN-047.c3 deeper_analysis discussion).

The biggest residual weakness is Hole 1: the lead claim conflates two distinct patterns in the eleven-WIN burst — (a) the five cosmological MOND-list items (047/048/052/054/055), and (b) the six other items (044/053 dome-internal derivations; 045/046/049/050/051 standard-physics tidal relabels). Both patterns are SC-grade, but they are SC for DIFFERENT reasons, and the prose's 'almost item-for-item' framing applies cleanly only to (a). A defender who clicks through and finds M2/S2/K1/O1/N2 tidal constituents listed as 'MOND-community talking points' has a legitimate gotcha. The fix is small (split the eleven into five-plus-six and note the parallel-but-distinct patterns); the structural argument survives intact.

Hole 2 is also straightforward to address: the public-record commit-burst timestamps (three commits at 04:57, 05:14, 05:29 UTC on 2026-03-23, 11 WINs total in 32 minutes) are strictly stronger evidence than the current bare 'imported in a single late-March commit sequence' assertion, and they do NOT require invoking the LLM-co-author footer detail (which the operator correctly kept in research-note context). Surfacing the commit-burst evidence converts the import claim from rhetorical assertion to documented fact.

The other holes (h3-h9) are tightenings rather than corrections: closing the third-option dome defense (illusory 2D firmament-pattern galaxies), citing a specific MOND-community paper that enumerates the canonical list, sharpening the kernel-of-truth pivot to match Part 6's established pattern, verifying the rim-height number, and optional TLDR/closing-paragraph polish.

Net recommendation: integrate with minor_edits addressing Hole 1 and Hole 2 mandatorily, Hole 3 and Hole 4 optionally. The section is publication-ready as a structural-analysis subsection of Part 6; the verdict reclassifications it documents are already in place and already adversarially reviewed.

Advocate-mode note: a sympathetic dome defender's strongest possible response is 'these aren't imports, they are independent corroboration, and the bounded-firmament reading is a strawman of the dome model's actual cosmological claims (which are silent on what lies beyond the firmament).' The prose's response — the dome's own published taxonomy contains no broader cosmology, only the disc/firmament/sun/moon parameters, and the silence-plus-explicit-bounded-parameters combination is positive contradiction not silence — is the correct response. The held-back commit-burst evidence (Hole 2) and the missing MOND-community-list citation (Hole 4) are the two places where this defense could currently get traction; both are fixable with small additions.

advocate_mode_notes: Strongest sympathetic-reader take: the section makes a clear structural observation (anomalies-list import without per-item derivation) and a clear physics argument (closed-world geometry forecloses galactic-scale referents). The verdict reclassifications are reasonable and the per-WIN prose already supports them. The dome defender's strongest available counter is to retreat to 'the bounded firmament is local, broader cosmology is unspecified' — which the EXP-295 anticipated_objections section addresses by pointing out the dome's unified-theory table makes the disc/firmament/sun/moon parameters the model's ENTIRE spatial geometry, with lambda_A = 4,283 Mpc as a relabeled Hubble length (cited via WIN-054 detail_evidence). This response is correct but is in the research-note layer, not in the published prose; whether to surface it is a judgment call.
cross_win_consistency: Section is consistent with the five underlying WIN reviews (WIN-047.c3, WIN-048.c4, WIN-052.c3, WIN-054.c3, WIN-055.c3) on file. The section's framework-level contradiction reading matches WIN-052.c3's 'physical structure cannot accommodate galaxies' framing and WIN-054.c3's 'inescapable dilemma' framing. Consistent with Part 6's existing pattern of cross-cutting structural observations (p6-612-11 cluster-closure pattern; p6-614-kappa-cluster cross-eclipse vise; p6-66-5 globe-values-relabeled-as-dome-predictions). Verdict label 'Self-Contradicted' for the five reclassified WINs is consistent with the existing Self-Contradicted application pattern (per WIN-029, 038, 044, 049, etc.: applied for both numerical/textual conflicts and framework-level coherence failures).
quantitative_verification: Verified by direct read of data/wins.json: WIN-047 verdict=Self-Contradicted (matches prose's reclassification claim); WIN-048 verdict=Self-Contradicted; WIN-052 verdict=Self-Contradicted; WIN-054 verdict=Self-Contradicted; WIN-055 verdict=Self-Contradicted. All five citations (Mistele 2024, McGaugh 2016, Asencio 2023, Kim 2021, Planck 2015 XXIII) match WIN-052 and WIN-054 detail_evidence verbatim. Triple firmament-height (8,537 / 9,086 / 9,572 km) verified in WIN-029 detail_evidence. Six-row killshot.html structure verified in data/kill-shots.json (7 entries, KS-007 marked 'our addition'). H(r=20,015) rim-value claim NOT verified — see Hole 6.
summary_for_decider: Section-new c1 review of EXP-295 proposed_subsection_html (Part 6.13 The MOND Grouping). Verdict: current verdict holds (the section is publication-ready), confidence 0.83, recommended_action=minor_edit. Two major holes (h1 overgeneralized eleven-vs-five lead claim conflating tidal/dome-internal WINs with MOND-list cosmological WINs; h2 held-back commit-burst evidence weakens the import-vs-convergence framing). Three moderate holes (h3 third-option dome defense not explicitly closed; h4 missing canonical-MOND-list citation; h5 kernel-of-truth pivot weaker than Part 6 pattern). Four minor holes (h6 rim-height number to verify; h7 id format note; h8 universal-quantifier generalization in closing; h9 dense TLDR). Five per-WIN reclassifications (047/048/052/054/055 -> Self-Contradicted) already applied and adversarially reviewed under WIN-047.c3 / WIN-048.c4 / WIN-052.c3 / WIN-054.c3 / WIN-055.c3. This review covers ONLY the cross-cutting section prose itself; the per-WIN audit trail is independent and on file. Recommend integration after addressing h1 and h2 (mandatory) — both are small textual additions, not structural rewrites.
